rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOSE ALFREDO ZERMENO II
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 06/08/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 5/23/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MICHAEL WALTON GONZALEZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 06/13/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Notice of the First Amended Petition has not been filed. 10/4/2019 Request for special notice needs to be sent to Elana Weinstein and Debra L. Kasper. Please give notice to Skootsky and Der. (Probate Code 1202). 2) The prior counsel Skootsky and Der has neither consented to the fee request nor joined in the petition for fees. 3) All pleadings filed in proceedings under the Probate Code must be verified. (CRC 7.103). 4) The petition for final distribution must state the date on which notice to the Franchise Tax Board was mailed pursuant to Probate Code 9202(c). [LRSF 14.35 I. 2)] 5) Please explain page 1 line 18 Exhibit, page 3 line 6 Exhibit, page 6 line 15 Exhibit, page 7 line 7 Exhibit. There are no exhibits attached to the petition. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 5/31/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF EDUARDO A. MARROQUIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 06/08/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Please explain the legal basis / cite legal authority for the account beginning 9/21/2020. Conservator was appointed 09/03/2020. It appears the account needs to be amended to begin 09/03/2020 the time of appointment. See Probate Code 2620(a). 2) Please explain why the I&A's have date of appointment as 09/30/2020 when the Conservator was appointed 09/03/2020. It appears the I&A need to be corrected to 09/03/2020. 3) The Inventory and Appraisal filed 01/08/2021 does not have the required attachment. Please re-file I&A with the required attachment. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 5/23/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 11th Amended General Order which took effect on 04/25/2022 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE BEALLO FAMILY TRUST DATED MAY 18, 1993 AS AMENDED
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Determination Of Incapacity Of Settlor; To Compel Production Of Estate Planning Documents And Trust Records; And To Compel Trustee'S Acco
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The Court to determine how best to proceed. No further proof has been filed that the trustee's primary residence is in San Francisco. The trustee's objection, filed on 12/16/21, has as Exhibit 1 a copy of the 16061.7 notification, showing Richard George's office address at 733 Front Street, Suite 502, San Francisco (but with his number being 530-363-2037). Is this sufficient to establish venue here? There is no proof that that notification was mailed to the beneficiaries. Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review the 11th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: Meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING MUST CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF OPAL DEAN KITCHIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/13/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: No courtesy copies. No proposed order. [LRSF 2.7 B., 14.7, and 14.59 E.] No proof of publication. A cursory review of the petition online (as a ONE-time courtesy) indicates that it is incomplete at items 5a(3) or (4) and 8. The petitioner (the person named in item 2 of the petition) is missing from item 8. How does a great nephew have priority for appointment? Has the surviving son declined to act and nominated him? Niblings are not heirs when issue survived. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 6/3/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
